new_classes.label_de: Gin in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von mehr als 2 Litern (KN 2208 50 19)
new_classes.definition_en: Gin, being a spirituous beverage generally produced from rectified cereal spirit or ethyl alcohol by simple or successive distillation using juniper berries and other aromatic substances, presented in containers of a nominal volume exceeding 2 litres, corresponding to CN subheading 2208 50 19.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria for CN 2208 50 19 are: (1) the product is Gin — a spirit produced from rectified cereal spirit or ethyl alcohol by distillation with juniper berries and other aromatic substances — captured via subclass of eucn:Gin and the JuniperBerryAromaticQuality and AddedFlavouringQuality restrictions; (2) it is presented in containers with a nominal volume exceeding 2 litres — captured via the BFO has_part some LargeContainer restriction; (3) it is disjoint from its sibling 2208 50 11 (containers ≤ 2 L, i.e. GinInSmallContainer) — expressed via the complement restriction. Minor gap: the production process (RectifiedCerealSpiritDistillationProcess) is defined in the TBox but not explicitly restricted here to avoid over-constraining (gin can also be made from neutral spirits of agricultural origin). The minimum alcoholic strength criterion (typically ≥ 37.5 % vol) is present in the TBox as MinAlcohol37_5VolQuality but is a generic Gin-level criterion already implicit in the parent class; it was not repeated here to avoid redundancy with the parent. The legal note text is truncated (ends mid-sentence), so some detail on admissible aromatic substances is unavailable.
